Combined Sewer Overflows - Part 3

Implementation and Deployment Strategy

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

Rolling Out Condition Monitoring

Implementing condition monitoring across a water utility requires careful planning. Start with the highest-value assets and work systematically across the system.

Phase 1: Assessment

Identify the most critical equipment. For combined sewer systems, this typically means the largest pumps and those most likely to fail during peak flow events.

Phase 2: Baseline Monitoring

Deploy sensors and establish baseline electrical signatures for healthy equipment. This baseline allows the system to detect deviations and degradation over time.

Phase 3: Integration

Integrate condition monitoring data with your maintenance management system. Automated alerts enable rapid response to developing faults.

Phase 4: Optimization

Use historical data to refine maintenance schedules and optimize the program based on actual equipment behavior and performance.

Key Success Factors

  • Executive support and commitment to predictive maintenance
  • Staff training on new systems and processes
  • Integration with existing maintenance workflows
  • Clear metrics for success and ROI tracking
